audit.promotion.agree_label:
Promotion rule:
- If all items in the current audit scope are verified as `{agree_tag}`, use the following promotion candidate for `## {next_task_section}` instead of generating one ad hoc.
- Promotion candidate source: `{source}`
- Current promotion candidate:
  - {next_task}
audit.eslint.mismatch_header: ⚠ eslint scope mismatch — check before audit:
audit.eslint.heading: {heading}
audit.no_pending: No {trigger} or {pending} items in claude.md. Skipping audit.
audit.session.resuming_last: Resuming most recent Codex session in this repo.
audit.session.reset: No remaining {tag} items — session reset for next audit.
audit.session.reset_pending: Pending items detected — resetting session to prevent orphan call accumulation.
respond.demoting: Removing {count} downgraded item(s) from claude.md agreed anchor:
respond.removed_next_task.dryrun: (dry-run) would remove deprecated '## {section}' section from claude.md.
respond.run_with_auto_fix: Run with --auto-fix to invoke claude -p for corrections.
respond.retro.all_clear:
[ACTION REQUIRED — CYCLE COMPLETION] All audit items resolved ({rx_id}).
Do not wait for user instruction — complete the full cycle immediately:

== Phase 1: Retrospective ==
1. Present what went well / what went wrong this cycle
2. Exchange feedback with the user (honest improvements, not praise)
3. Extract repeatable principles from feedback → save to memory
4. Clean up memory — remove duplicate/stale entries

== Phase 2: Commit + Handoff ==
5. echo session-self-improvement-complete (gate clears)
6. Commit changes (git add → commit)
7. Update handoff — memory/session_handoff.md

== Phase 3: Next Task ==
8. Check audit verdict for next task and start immediately
9. Implement → submit evidence → audit → loop continues
respond.retro.skipped_is_retro: Last synced item was a retrospective ({rx_id}) — skipping auto retrospective.
respond.retro.skipped_no_script: retro_script not configured — skipping auto retrospective.
retro.marker_set: [retro] Retrospective marker set ({rx_id}) — retrospective will start automatically on next tool use.
index.format.glob_in_test: [FORMAT_GLOB_IN_TEST] Test Command contains glob patterns. Use an explicit file list.
index.format.empty_result: [FORMAT_EMPTY_RESULT] Test Result is empty or too short. Copy-paste the terminal output.
index.format.check_header:
[FORMAT-CHECK] Evidence package format error — skipping audit.
Fix the following items and resubmit:

{errors}

Error code: FORMAT_INCOMPLETE
index.quick_audit.no_backtick_paths: [QUICK_AUDIT] No backtick file paths in Changed Files. Use `path/to/file.ts` format.
index.quick_audit.not_in_diff: [QUICK_AUDIT] `{file}` is listed in Changed Files but not in git diff
index.quick_audit.tag_conflict: [QUICK_AUDIT] Tag conflict: {trigger} and {agree} on the same line
index.quick_audit.header:
[QUICK AUDIT] Pre-flight check found {count} warning(s):
{warnings}

These items are likely to be flagged by the auditor as well. Fixing before submission saves audit cost.
Audit continues.

index.audit.already_running: [quorum] Audit already running (PID {pid}). Skipping duplicate execution.
index.timestamp.label: Audit completed
gate.protocol:
[ACTION REQUIRED — CYCLE COMPLETION]
Audit cycle completed. Do not wait for user instruction — complete the full cycle immediately.

## Agreed Items
{context}

## Phase 1: Retrospective (Bash blocked — Read/Write/Edit only)
1. Present what went well / what went wrong this cycle
2. Exchange feedback with the user (honest improvements, not praise)
3. Extract repeatable principles from feedback → save to memory
4. Clean up memory — remove duplicate/stale entries

## Phase 2: Commit + Handoff (echo clears Bash)
5. echo session-self-improvement-complete
6. Commit changes (git add → commit)
7. Update handoff — memory/session_handoff.md

## Phase 3: Next Task
8. Check audit verdict for next task and start immediately
9. Implement → submit evidence → audit → loop continues
gate.blocked:
[SESSION GATE] Retrospective protocol incomplete — {tool} blocked.
Use Read/Write/Edit for memory cleanup, then run 'echo session-self-improvement-complete'.
subagent.stop.deferred_retro:
[ACTION REQUIRED — DEFERRED RETROSPECTIVE]
The implementer session triggered retrospective ({rx_id}) but deferred it to this session.
Agreed items:
{items}

Complete the retrospective protocol in this session:
1. Review what went well / what went wrong
2. Exchange feedback with user
3. Save repeatable principles to memory
4. echo session-self-improvement-complete
pdoc.title: # Feedback Promotion Index
pdoc.meta: > Status: `active` | Type: feedback-loop promotion rule | Generated by: `.claude/quorum respond`
pdoc.purpose_body:
When the current audit track in `{feedback_glob}` is fully closed as `{agree_tag}`,
the next work item is promoted automatically from this document instead of being written ad hoc.

This document is **generated automatically** from `feedback-promotion.plan.json`,
the current verdicts in `{gpt_file}`, and the `## {agreed_anchor}` anchor
inside `{watch_file}`.
pdoc.rule_body:
- If the current audited scope is fully closed as `{agree_tag}`, `{gpt_file}` should use the first item under `## {promotion_target}` as `## {next_task}`.
- Residual `{pending_tag}` audit items in `{feedback_glob}` do not automatically block promotion of the next improved work item for an already agreed track.
